herb_parts.name: root
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus root, also known as Huang Qi in traditional Chinese medicine, has been used to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome. The root contains compounds such as astragalosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Astragalus membranaceus root has also been traditionally used to enhance the body's natural defenses and support overall health.

herb_parts.name: rhizome
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus rhizome, also known as Huang Qi, contains polysaccharides and flavonoids that exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The rhizome has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to enhance the body's resistance to disease and to promote overall health. Its bioactive compounds have been shown to modulate the immune system, making it a potential treatment for various conditions, including autoimmune diseases and viral infections.

herb_parts.name: leaf
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus leaf is traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various conditions, including fever and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The leaf is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which can help protect against oxidative stress and infections. Furthermore, the leaf is thought to have a role in improving the body's immune function and enhancing its ability to fight off diseases.

herb_parts.name: whole plant
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus whole plant contains various medicinal compounds, including flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides. These compounds have been found to exhibit immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, contributing to the plant's potential health benefits. The whole plant is used in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance the immune system and improve overall health.

herb_parts.name: stem
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus stem, also known as Huang Qi stem, is used in traditional Chinese medicine to tonify the Qi and boost the immune system. The stem is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. It is typically harvested in the summer months and then dried for later use in herbal remedies.

herb_parts.name: aerial part
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus aerial part contains flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which are believed to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in this part may also exhibit antioxidant and anti-fatigue effects, contributing to its traditional use in traditional Chinese medicine. Additionally, studies have shown that the aerial part of Astragalus membranaceus may help to improve cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.

herb_parts.name: flower
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus flower is not commonly cited as a medicinal part, but it may contain some bioactive compounds. However, the root of Astragalus membranaceus is more widely recognized for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The root's bioactive compounds, such as polysaccharides and saponins, are thought to contribute to its medicinal benefits.

herb_parts.name: young stem
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus young stem is used in traditional Chinese medicine and contains various bioactive compounds. It is known to have immunomodulatory effects due to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides. The bioactive compounds in the young stem are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

herb_parts.name: fruit
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus fruit is not commonly used in traditional medicine, but its seeds are considered beneficial for various health issues. However, some studies suggest that astragalus seeds may have a role in promoting diuresis and aiding in the treatment of certain urinary problems. The seeds of Astragalus membranaceus may also have a laxative effect due to their saponin content.

herb_parts.name: old stem
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus old stem, also known as Huang Qi, is valued for its medicinal properties. The old stem of Astragalus membranaceus is rich in polysaccharides, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. These compounds are thought to help boost the immune system and have potential benefits in the treatment of various diseases.

herb_parts.name: seed
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus seed, particularly the embryo and endosperm, contains flavonoids and saponins that have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to enhance immune function. The seed also contains astragaloside IV, a triterpenoid saponin with potential anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. These compounds have been studied for their potential to boost the body's natural defense mechanisms and protect against infections.

herb_parts.name: petal
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus petal is known to contain astragaloside IV, which has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties. The petal is also a rich source of flavonoids, such as kaempferol and isorhapontigenin, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Additionally, the petal of Astragalus membranaceus has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma, due to its purported expectorant properties.

herb_parts.name: bark
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus bark, particularly the inner layer, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine due to its high content of astragalosides and other bioactive compounds. The bark's polysaccharides and flavonoids are believed to contribute to its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. Research suggests that astragalus membranaceus bark may have potential therapeutic applications in promoting cardiovascular health and modulating the immune system.

herb_parts.name: stamen
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus stamen contains flavonoids, which have been reported to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects. The flavonoids present in the stamen of Astragalus membranaceus are believed to contribute to its traditional use in Chinese medicine for enhancing the immune system. Additionally, the stamen's flavonoids may also have antioxidant properties, helping to protect against oxidative stress.

herb_parts.name: stem bark
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus stem bark is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The stem bark of Astragalus membranaceus also contains saponins, which have been found to exhibit immunomodulatory effects. These bioactive compounds in the stem bark of Astragalus membranaceus may contribute to its potential health benefits, including enhanced immune function and reduced oxidative stress.

herb_parts.name: leaf bud
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus leaf bud, also known as Huangqi in traditional Chinese medicine, contains flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides that exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds have been found to enhance the activity of natural killer cells and T-lymphocytes, promoting immune system function. Additionally, the leaf bud of Astragalus membranaceus has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including fatigue, cough, and respiratory infections.

herb_parts.name: flower bud
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus flower bud is a medicinal component that contains flavonoids and saponins. These compounds have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to the herb's traditional uses in promoting immune function and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents in the flower bud may also exhibit cardiovascular benefits and protect against cellular damage.

herb_parts.name: leaf tip
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus leaf tip contains flavonoids, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It also contains phenolic acids and polysaccharides that may contribute to its immunomodulatory effects. These bioactive compounds may help to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress.

herb_parts.name: leaf base
herb_parts.desc: Astragalus membranaceus leaf base is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. The leaf base of this plant is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance the body's resistance to disease and promote overall well-being. It is also thought to have antiviral and antibacterial effects, which can help to protect against infections and promote healing.
